deep_dive.title: HiFuzz: Hierarchical Reinforcement Learning for Semantic-Aware and Adaptive CPU Fuzzing
deep_dive.abstract: Modern processor verification struggles to reach deep architectural states due to the inefficiencies of traditional mutation-based fuzzing. We propose HiFuzz, a novel hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that replaces mutation with a structured, two-layer generation process: a Program Agent for global layout and a Basic Block Agent for precise instruction filling. To overcome reward sparsity, HiFuzz integrates an adaptive coverage reward mechanism and a semantic-aware basic block encoder providing intrinsic feedback. Extensive evaluations on three real-world RISC-V cores demonstrate that HiFuzz significantly outperforms state-of-the-art fuzzers in coverage and bug detection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: hierarchical RL for CPU fuzzing on RISC-V cores
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于RISC-V处理器核模糊测试的分层强化学习框架
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于RISC-V处理器核模糊测试的分层强化学习框架
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于RISC-V处理器核模糊测试的分层强化学习框架
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HiFuzz uses hierarchical RL to generate CPU instructions for fuzzing, outperforming state-of-the-art fuzzers on real RISC-V cores.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统变异型模糊测试难以抵达处理器深层架构状态的问题，提出了HiFuzz，一个采用分层强化学习的语义感知自适应CPU模糊测试框架。HiFuzz通过程序代理和基本块代理双层生成过程，结合自适应覆盖奖励和语义编码器，在三个真实RISC-V核上显著提升了覆盖率和漏洞检测能力，为硬件模糊测试提供了高效的新方案。

deep_dive.title: LLM Assisted Verification Assertion Generation: Challenges and Future Directions
deep_dive.abstract: Assertion-based Verification (ABV) plays a critical role in the Design Verification (DV) process. However, ABV requires substantial manual effort in generating assertion from specification by verification engineers, making it a time-consuming stage in the chip design flow. With the recent development of Large Language Models (LLMs), researchers have started exploring their use as an assistance in the ABV process, particularly for generating SystemVerilog Assertions (SVAs) from design specification. In this paper, we provide an overview of recent works, highlighting the different methods used to generate SVAs. In particular, we investigate LLM-based SVA generation and ask a central question: How can LLM-based assertion generation be made systematic and quality-aware? While addressing this key question, we provide Key Takeaways at the end of each challenge, summarizing the important methodological insights, and also provide guidelines and directions in solving those challenges that can help generate a high-quality set of assertions using LLMs.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-based SystemVerilog assertion generation for RTL verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于LLM的SystemVerilog断言生成用于RTL验证
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于LLM的SystemVerilog断言生成用于RTL验证
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于LLM的SystemVerilog断言生成用于RTL验证
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Explores LLM-assisted generation of SystemVerilog assertions from design specifications, highlighting methods and challenges for systematic adoption in design verification.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文综述了利用大语言模型从设计规范自动生成SystemVerilog断言的最新研究。断言是RTL形式验证的关键输入，但手工编写耗时耗力。论文探讨了LLM辅助断言生成的不同方法，分析了其有效性，并指出了实现系统性应用面临的挑战与未来方向，对硬件形式验证具有重要参考价值。

deep_dive.title: ESBMC-Arduino: Closing the Deployment Gap for Formal Verification of Open-Hardware PLCs
deep_dive.abstract: OpenPLC, Arduino OPTA, CONTROLLINO, and Industrial Shields M-Duino bring IEC 61131-3 to low-cost microcontrollers used in real automation and industrial control system (ICS) security research. Existing open-source verifiers for IEC 61131-3, including ESBMC-PLC, prove safety over an abstract scan-cycle model with idealized unbounded integers. The board artifact runs on a resource-constrained microcontroller unit (MCU) with 16-bit words (8-bit AVR Arduinos), and sensors are read via a finite-resolution analog-to-digital converter (ADC). We show this deployment gap makes naive width-aware verification unsound: across 123 real programs, checking 16-bit overflow without a hardware input model yields 44% false alarms (54/123) and finds no genuine defects, because it explores sensor values no ADC can produce. Since the gap lies where computation meets the physical process - a bounded sensor reading scaled by finite-width arithmetic into an actuation command - an overflow can silently suppress a safety action, such as a high-level alarm. An unbounded input model fabricates alarms that no environment can trigger. We present hardware-faithful verification for IEC 61131-3 on open hardware: a declarative hardware abstraction layer (HAL) descriptor (width, ADC/PWM resolution, I/O binding) and a sound lowering that interprets arithmetic at target width and constrains inputs to hardware-realizable ranges. We instantiate it for Arduino as ArduinoTool, deriving HAL parameters from official cores and realizing the input-range model in the ESBMC Ladder Diagram (LD) frontend. On the 123-program corpus, the HAL annotator eliminates all 54 false alarms while preserving robustness proofs, and a controlled corpus demonstrates the rare width-dependent defects it detects with realizable witnesses.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Formal verification of open-hardware PLCs using ESBMC with symbolic execution
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用ESBMC符号执行对开源硬件PLC进行形式验证
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用ESBMC符号执行对开源硬件PLC进行形式验证
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用ESBMC符号执行对开源硬件PLC进行形式验证
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: ESBMC-Arduino addresses the deployment gap by accounting for hardware constraints (16-bit words, ADC resolution), reducing false alarms from 44% to near zero.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对开源硬件PLC（如Arduino）形式验证的部署鸿沟，扩展ESBMC以考虑16位字长和有限分辨率ADC等硬件约束。在123个实际程序上，原生宽度感知验证产生44%假警报，而ESBMC-Arduino消除了假警报且未遗漏错误，显著提高了硬件形式验证的实用性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对开源硬件PLC（如Arduino）形式验证的部署鸿沟，扩展ESBMC以考虑16位字长和有限分辨率ADC等硬件约束。在123个实际程序上，原生宽度感知验证产生44%假警报，而ESBMC-Arduino消除了假警报且未遗漏错误，显著提高了硬件形式验证的实用性。

deep_dive.title: Separation Logic for Memory Conflict Detection in High-Level Synthesis
deep_dive.abstract: High-Level Synthesis leverages loop unrolling and array partitioning, but scheduling concurrent accesses is challenging when indices contain non-affine arithmetic. Conventional polyhedral frameworks systematically over-approximate these non-linear transformations, forcing conservative serialization that degrades performance. To minimize this bottleneck, we present a spatial verification framework operating at the LLVM Intermediate Representation (IR) level. By extracting flat arithmetic expressions from "getelementptr" instructions, it models memory banks as polymorphic spatial predicates to handle non-affine terms. Structural safety is enforced via a Conflict-Free Unrolling condition using Separation Logic's separating conjunction; concurrent operations targeting the same bank trigger an automatic spatial contradiction. This disjointness requirement is reduced to a matrix of pairwise inequalities over immutable Static Single Assignment (SSA) variables for a Satisfiability Modulo Theories (SMT) oracle. To guarantee safety against undecidable non-linear arithmetic, we implement a deterministic sequential fallback. Finally, a theorem of soundness bridges algebraic SMT verification with Register Transfer Level trace safety, ensuring physical hardware immune to structural memory collisions.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: separation logic for memory conflict detection in high-level synthesis verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 在高层次综合验证中使用分离逻辑检测内存冲突
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 在高层次综合验证中使用分离逻辑检测内存冲突
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 在高层次综合验证中使用分离逻辑检测内存冲突
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents a spatial verification framework using separation logic to detect memory conflicts in high-level synthesis, improving performance by avoiding conservative serialization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对高层次综合中循环展开和数组分区导致的内存访问冲突问题，提出了一种基于分离逻辑的空间验证框架。通过从LLVM IR中提取表达式并建模内存库为多态空间谓词，框架有效处理非仿射项，避免了传统方法的保守序列化，提升了并发执行效率。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对高层次综合中循环展开和数组分区导致的内存访问冲突问题，提出了一种基于分离逻辑的空间验证框架。通过从LLVM IR中提取表达式并建模内存库为多态空间谓词，框架有效处理非仿射项，避免了传统方法的保守序列化，提升了并发执行效率。

deep_dive.title: Harnessing Code Agents for Automatic Software Verification
deep_dive.abstract: Formal verification offers the strongest guarantee of software correctness, but it does not scale: the proofs demanded by interactive theorem provers such as Coq require enormous expert effort. Large language models (LLMs) promise to generate these proofs automatically, yet existing approaches wire a fixed, human-designed proof strategy into the system and constrain the model to follow it (retrieving premises and predicting tactics one step at a time, or splitting goals by divide-and-conquer), and still prove only a fraction of their target theorems.   We show that imposing such a strategy is unnecessary and limiting. Handing the whole lemma to a general LLM code agent (for example, Claude Code), free to choose its own approach, and wrapping it in a verification harness is both simpler and more effective, achieving full coverage: every targeted lemma proved, with no failures and no Coq expert intervention. The agent writes the proofs under feedback and hard constraints from the harness that keep each one sound (accepted only when the prover's kernel closes it), complete (no obligation left unproved or silently dropped), and terminating (no divergent tactics).   We evaluate this harness plus code agent along three dimensions. (1) Core logic: on Iris, the state-of-the-art separation logic for concurrent and memory-manipulating programs, Aria proves all 4,257 lemmas of the four core modules and the 217 lemmas verifying Rust's standard libraries built on it, fully automatically. (2) Comparison with prior LLM provers: on reglang, where prior provers manage barely one in eight, Aria proves all 318. (3) Generality: on iris-lean, the unfinished Lean 4 port of Iris, it proves 72 not-yet-ported lemmas, showing the approach is not specific to Coq. A state-of-the-art model (Claude Opus 4.7) can write proofs for verified software development fully and automatically.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM code agents for automatic theorem proving in formal verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用LLM代码智能体自动定理证明进行形式验证
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用LLM代码智能体自动定理证明进行形式验证
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用LLM代码智能体自动定理证明进行形式验证
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Shows that general LLM code agents can automate theorem proving in Coq, achieving higher proof rates than specialized approaches, promising for hardware formal verification transfer.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有LLM自动证明方法受限于固定策略，无法充分发挥模型能力。本文提出使用通用LLM代码智能体（如Claude Code）自由选择策略进行定理证明，在Coq基准上取得了更高的证明率。该方法可迁移至硬件形式验证，如模型检查和定理证明，为LLM辅助硬件设计验证开辟了新途径。

deep_dive.title: Miter-Aware LUT Mapping: Aligning Structure and Solvability for Efficient Logic Equivalence Checking
deep_dive.abstract: Logic Equivalence Checking (LEC), a fundamental hardware verification task, is often bottlenecked by synthesis-induced structural perturbations and XOR-dense regions that degrade SAT solver performance. We contend that the modeling of the miter is as critical as the SAT solver itself. To this end, we introduce a miter-aware mapping framework that strategically formulates the problem before solving. By constructing a LUT-based miter -- instead of a traditional, flat netlist -- our approach preserves critical structural correspondence between the two designs while making high-level logic relations explicit. Our framework uniquely integrates three techniques: equivalence-preserving mapping to structurally align the two circuits, Gaussian-guided XOR modeling to algebraically simplify dense arithmetic, and solver-oriented LUT selection to generate a representation optimized for efficient SAT reasoning. Evaluated on comprehensive datasets, our method achieves up to a \textbf{92.1\%} reduction across state-of-the-art SAT solvers. This demonstrates that a solver-aware modeling paradigm, which unifies structural mapping with SAT reasoning, can fundamentally enhance LEC efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: miter-aware LUT mapping for logic equivalence checking in hardware verification
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向硬件验证中逻辑等价性检查的Miter感知LUT映射
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向硬件验证中逻辑等价性检查的Miter感知LUT映射
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向硬件验证中逻辑等价性检查的Miter感知LUT映射
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a miter-aware LUT mapping framework that improves SAT solver performance for logic equivalence checking, preserving structural correspondence between designs.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对逻辑等价性检查中SAT求解器由于结构扰动而性能下降的问题，提出了一种Miter感知的LUT映射框架。通过构建基于LUT的miter而非传统扁平网表，框架保留了设计间的结构对应关系，使高层逻辑关系显式化，显著提升了等价性检查效率。实验表明该方法在工业基准测试上有效。

deep_dive.title: Detecting Ladder Logic Bombs in IEC 61131-3 PLC Programs using ESBMC-PLC+: A Formal Verification Approach with Trigger Synthesis
deep_dive.abstract: A Ladder Logic Bomb (LLB) is malicious control logic in a Programmable Logic Controller (PLC) program that lies dormant until a trigger activates a payload to manipulate actuators, forge sensor readings, or deny operator control. We observe that real malicious logic hides inside function-block bodies, which existing ladder-diagram verifiers drop from their intermediate representation (IR), making bombs invisible to provers. We present ESBMC-LLB, which uses ESBMC-PLC+ as its verification engine and adds a modeling layer that exposes function-block logic and recasts bomb detection as a formal verification problem: a scan-watchdog exposes non-termination payloads, and output wiring exposes actuator-forgery payloads as safety violations. k-induction gives an unbounded proof of bomb-absence across all scans, and the bounded model checker returns a counterexample that is the trigger - guarantees that signature, anomaly, and CFG-triage detectors lack. On the public Iacobelli 2024 dataset, ESBMC-LLB detects all 30 bombs and recovers every trigger; it also detects adaptive triggers (computed, opaque-arithmetic, multi-scan) that evade CFG-triage. We also report the first semantic model-checker evaluation on PLC-Defuser's SWaT corpus: our analog extension makes the full corpus parseable; on v1.0.0, it detects 149/150 bombs (99%) with zero false positives, recovering each trigger; on a later version with nonlinear non-termination bombs, detection drops to 49% as the SMT solver times out. We conclude that semantic model checking and CFG-triage are complementary - the former gives unbounded proofs, adaptive-trigger robustness, and handles Boolean/integer and linear analog logic; the latter leads to nonlinear analog non-termination, and we delineate where each wins.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: formal verification of PLC hardware programs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 对PLC硬件程序的形式化验证
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 对PLC硬件程序的形式化验证
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 对PLC硬件程序的形式化验证
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents ESBMC-LLB, a formal verification approach to detect ladder logic bombs in PLC programs by exposing function-block logic and using trigger synthesis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对PLC梯形逻辑炸弹这一安全威胁，提出ESBMC-LLB形式化验证方法。该方法通过建模层暴露隐藏的函数块逻辑，将炸弹检测转化为形式化验证问题，利用扫描看门狗和输出接线条件合成触发条件。实验表明该方法能有效检测恶意控制逻辑，弥补了现有梯形图验证器忽略函数块体的缺陷，提升了工业控制系统的安全性。

quick_skim.title: RTL Fault Injection of a Deployed Graph Neural Network Trigger for Belle II
quick_skim.abstract: As particle physics detectors grow in scale, High Energy Physics experiments must process ever-increasing data volumes. Level-1 trigger systems, implemented on Field-Programmable Gate Arrays and increasingly using neural-network algorithms, filter this data in real time. However, their proximity to the interaction point exposes them to radiation, which can corrupt outputs, stall processing pipelines, or damage hardware, with significant financial and scientific consequences. In this work, we present the first Register Transfer Level fault-injection study of a deployed Level-1 hardware neural-network trigger, GNN-ETM in the Belle II trigger system. We target three failure modes most consequential to a real-time trigger pipeline: deadlocks, timeouts, and packet-integrity violations. Through two complementary campaigns, we inject 1 442 840 Single-Event Upsets across 211 245 signals. We find a monitoring asymmetry in the existing verification infrastructure and propose inter-stage liveness monitoring as a more accurate alternative to output-only observation, showing that Mean Time To Failure estimates from the two approaches differ by up to 78.7%. The resulting per-stage data identifies the highest-priority hardening targets.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RTL fault injection for hardware verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对硬件验证的RTL故障注入
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 针对硬件验证的RTL故障注入
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对硬件验证的RTL故障注入
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Presents the first RTL fault-injection study of a deployed hardware neural-network trigger, analyzing deadlocks, timeouts, and output corruption under radiation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文首次对Belle II实验中部署的硬件神经网络触发器GNN-ETM进行寄存器传输级故障注入研究。针对辐射环境可能导致的死锁、超时和输出损坏三种故障模式，在RTL层面注入故障并分析其对实时触发流水线的影响。研究表明该触发器在部分故障场景下仍能保持功能，但存在特定脆弱点，为高能物理实验中的容错设计提供了重要参考。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文首次对Belle II实验中部署的硬件神经网络触发器GNN-ETM进行寄存器传输级故障注入研究。针对辐射环境可能导致的死锁、超时和输出损坏三种故障模式，在RTL层面注入故障并分析其对实时触发流水线的影响。研究表明该触发器在部分故障场景下仍能保持功能，但存在特定脆弱点，为高能物理实验中的容错设计提供了重要参考。

quick_skim.title: Mechanism-level routing failure in LLMs over Lean-verified algebraic structures
quick_skim.abstract: We present an empirical study of structural routing failure in large language models (LLMs) over a formally verified algebraic corpus. The task requires selecting the correct proof-mechanism label from a fixed closed template set for compact mathematical objects drawn from the FiberRing formalization in Lean 4, where each item is anchored to a Lean-verified artifact and assigned a label from the corresponding certificate family.   Our central finding is a mechanism-level routing ceiling: under blind conditions, gpt-oss-120b achieves 80.3% template accuracy on 22 FiberRing items (n=66; temperature=0, seed=0), while Llama 3.3 70B reaches 68.2%. Exposing a mechanism-bearing Lean verdict/witness cue (Condition A2) raises accuracy to 90.9% and 81.8% -- gaps of +10.6 and +13.6 pp termed cue-induced routing uplift.   The dominant failure is a CRT-to-ring-equivalence misroute: gpt-oss-120b misroutes 7 of 12 CRT items (58.3%) blind, zero under A2. A cross-model dissociation in Llama is notable: verdict accuracy is identical in both conditions (95.5%), while template accuracy improves 13.6 pp -- confirming that truth inference and proof-mechanism classification are separable capacities.   A cross-corpus extension (Set B; 6 POM/CollisionKernel items, 72 evaluations) provides a small cross-module check: CRT-granularity compression reappears with different labels, and an inverse cross-model dissociation emerges. These findings extend the router hypothesis (Cazares 2026) to formal algebraic structures. The full pipeline, manifest, and results are at https://github.com/bytepro-ai/fiber-routing-eval.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: LLMs evaluated on Lean-verified algebraic proof task
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: LLM在Lean验证的代数证明任务上评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: LLM在Lean验证的代数证明任务上评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: LLM在Lean验证的代数证明任务上评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: LLMs show mechanism-level routing failure on Lean-verified algebraic structures: accuracy reaches 80.3% for gpt-oss-120b and 68.2% for Llama 3.3 70B, improving with witness cues to 90.9% and 81.8% respectively.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对LLM在正式验证代数语料上的结构性路由失败进行实证研究。任务要求从固定模板中选择正确证明机制标签，基于Lean 4的FiberRing形式化。结果显示，在盲测条件下，gpt-oss-120b准确率达80.3%，Llama 3.3 70B达68.2%；暴露机制相关的Lean判决/见证线索后，准确率分别提升至90.9%和81.8%。该研究揭示了LLM在形式验证任务中的能力与局限，对硬件形式验证中运用LLM具有参考价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对LLM在正式验证代数语料上的结构性路由失败进行实证研究。任务要求从固定模板中选择正确证明机制标签，基于Lean 4的FiberRing形式化。结果显示，在盲测条件下，gpt-oss-120b准确率达80.3%，Llama 3.3 70B达68.2%；暴露机制相关的Lean判决/见证线索后，准确率分别提升至90.9%和81.8%。该研究揭示了LLM在形式验证任务中的能力与局限，对硬件形式验证中运用LLM具有参考价值。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Can LLMs assist in model checking and theorem proving for hardware design?

quick_skim.title: Towards Quantifier-Free Interpolation in Array Languages with Unbounded Data Specifications
quick_skim.abstract: We investigate quantifier-free interpolation properties for several fragments generalising the extensional theory of arrays. Our results include the (general) quantifier-free interpolation properties of combinatory array logic with iterated diffs and the uniform interpolation property of the simple flat array fragment. To our knowledge, these are the first positive quantifier-free interpolation results obtained for theories of arrays featuring expressive specifications over unbounded domains.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: quantifier-free interpolation for array theories, foundational for hardware verification of memories
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 数组理论的无量词插值，为硬件内存验证提供理论基础
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 数组理论的无量词插值，为硬件内存验证提供理论基础
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 数组理论的无量词插值，为硬件内存验证提供理论基础
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates quantifier-free interpolation for array theories with unbounded data specifications, proving first positive results that enable more expressive hardware verification tools.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 在硬件形式验证中，数组结构常用于建模内存，插值是重要的验证技术。本文研究了多种数组语言片段的无量词插值性质，首次证明了具有无界数据规范数组理论的正定量词自由插值结果。这些成果为硬件内存验证提供了理论支撑，可提升自动化验证能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 在硬件形式验证中，数组结构常用于建模内存，插值是重要的验证技术。本文研究了多种数组语言片段的无量词插值性质，首次证明了具有无界数据规范数组理论的正定量词自由插值结果。这些成果为硬件内存验证提供了理论支撑，可提升自动化验证能力。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: hardware verification techniques for digital circuits

quick_skim.title: SMART: A Machine Learning and Monte Carlo Framework for Rapid Analysis of Stochastic Transistor Aging and Process Variation in Digital Circuits
quick_skim.abstract: As CMOS technology scales into the deep nanometer regime, digital circuit reliability is increasingly threatened by the combined stochastic effects of Bias Temperature Instability (BTI) and Process Variation (PV). Traditional reliability analysis methods, which rely on computationally intensive simulations or extensive lookup tables, fail to scale efficiently for large designs, creating a critical bottleneck in design space exploration. To address this, we propose SMART, a novel framework that integrates Machine Learning (ML) with Monte Carlo simulation to enable rapid, high-fidelity reliability analysis. SMART employs Random Forest regression to predict gate delay distributions directly, bypassing time-consuming atomic model parameter extractions. Crucially, the model utilizes Bayesian Optimization for automated hyperparameter tuning, ensuring maximum predictive robustness across diverse libraries. Experimental validation on ISCAS85 benchmark circuits demonstrates that SMART achieves a 94.54% reduction in analysis time compared to state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining a remarkable average accuracy error of just 1.63%. By shifting computational complexity to an offline training phase, the proposed framework offers a scalable, accurate solution for designing resilient, reliability-aware digital systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: ML applied to digital circuit reliability analysis, a form of hardware verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 机器学习用于数字电路可靠性分析，属于硬件验证范畴
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 机器学习用于数字电路可靠性分析，属于硬件验证范畴
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 机器学习用于数字电路可靠性分析，属于硬件验证范畴
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SMART integrates Random Forest regression with Monte Carlo simulation to accelerate stochastic transistor aging and process variation analysis in digital circuits, enabling efficient design space exploration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 传统数字电路可靠性分析方法依赖大量仿真或查找表，在大规模设计下效率低下。本文提出SMART框架，将机器学习（随机森林回归）与蒙特卡洛模拟相结合，通过预测门延迟分布来快速分析晶体管老化和工艺变化。实验表明，SMART在保持高精度的同时显著加速了分析过程，为设计空间探索提供了高效工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 传统数字电路可靠性分析方法依赖大量仿真或查找表，在大规模设计下效率低下。本文提出SMART框架，将机器学习（随机森林回归）与蒙特卡洛模拟相结合，通过预测门延迟分布来快速分析晶体管老化和工艺变化。实验表明，SMART在保持高精度的同时显著加速了分析过程，为设计空间探索提供了高效工具。
